title: Wind-Tunnel Sensor Scaling Law — extrapolating to large compute & resolution
strategies: constant baseline: predict the mean training calibration error (ignores structure) -> ~0.1

log-linear power law: fit log L ~ c0 + c1 log C + c2 log R and emit A*C^c1*R^c2; captures the cheap-regime slope but has NO floor, so it collapses toward 0 in the large-compute held-out region and extrapolates poorly
floored scaling law: grid-search the two exponents (alpha,beta), least-squares fit E_inf + A*C^-alpha + B*R^-beta; identifies the irreducible floor and extrapolates well
alternatives: continuous/finer exponent optimisation, joint nonlinear least squares, adding a small cross term or ridge regularisation to trade complexity penalty against held-out error
